name: Beef and Broccoli with Garlic Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim fat from beef steak and cut into 2-inch strips. Cut each strip crosswise into 1/8-inch slices.
2. Toss the beef, vegetable oil, salt, and white pepper in a bowl. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
3. Peel the outer layer from broccoli stems. Cut stems lengthwise into 1-inch pieces and remove florets.
4. Place broccoli in boiling water. Cook until boiling, then cover and cook for 2 minutes. Drain immediately and rinse with cold water. Drain again.
5. Mix cornstarch, sesame oil, and chicken broth.
6. Heat a 12-inch skillet or wok until very hot. Add vegetable oil and coat the bottom of the pan.
7. Add beef to the skillet and stir-fry for 2 minutes or until browned. Remove beef from the skillet.
8. Return skillet to heat and add more oil. Rotate to coat the bottom.
9. Add garlic, ginger root, and bean sauce; stir-fry for 30 seconds.
10. Add bamboo shoots and stir-fry for 1 minute.
11. Stir in the remaining beef and broccoli. Cook with the cornstarch mixture for an additional 15 seconds or until thickened.
